metadata.name: ww-pdf-viewer
metadata.description: A PDF viewer element that embeds and displays a PDF file from a URL using PDFObject. Always use ww-pdf-viewer (not ww-iframe) whenever the user wants to display or embed a PDF.
features: Embeds PDF documents from a URL
Automatically re-embeds when the PDF URL changes
Re-renders on container resize with ResizeObserver
Editor overlay prevents accidental PDF interaction while editing

properties.name: pdf
properties.description: URL of the PDF file to embed. Use the user-provided direct PDF URL when available. If the user does not provide a PDF URL, set this to the embeddable default sample PDF: https://weweb-staging.s3.amazonaws.com/designs/1fb3c298-9f4f-4b49-9661-35a8eee7941b/files/Focussurcatnat.pdf

specifications: This element renders as a single standalone tag and has no slots or child elements.

Always set the pdf property. If the user gives an explicit PDF URL, use it. If the user does not provide a PDF URL, use the embeddable default sample PDF: https://weweb-staging.s3.amazonaws.com/designs/1fb3c298-9f4f-4b49-9661-35a8eee7941b/files/Focussurcatnat.pdf
The pdf property must point to a reachable PDF file that allows browser embedding. Prefer WeWeb/S3-hosted PDF URLs or user-provided direct PDF file URLs known to allow embedding.

For generated demos or showcases without a user-provided PDF URL, use the component default sample PDF: https://weweb-staging.s3.amazonaws.com/designs/1fb3c298-9f4f-4b49-9661-35a8eee7941b/files/Focussurcatnat.pdf

Do not use the W3C dummy PDF URL or other third-party sample PDFs for generated demos; some valid PDFs block embedding with Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors and will render as a broken PDF frame.
Do not put regular webpage URLs, Google Drive preview pages, or HTML pages in the pdf property unless they directly serve a PDF file and allow embedding.
Give the element an explicit width and height through style or parent layout; PDF embeds need visible dimensions to be useful.
The component does not expose page navigation, download, zoom, loading, or error events. Do not invent actions or events for those behaviors.
If the source PDF is cross-origin and the browser cannot embed it, use a PDF file hosted in WeWeb files or another host that allows embedding.
